One day on Venus is longer than one year on Venus. It takes Venus longer to rotate once on its axis than it does to complete one orbit around the Sun.
Space is completely silent because there is no atmosphere for sound waves to travel through.
A full NASA space suit costs around $12 million, with 70% of the cost for the backpack and control module.
Neutron stars are so dense that a single teaspoon of their material would weigh about 6 billion tons on Earth.
There are more trees on Earth (approx. 3 trillion) than stars in the Milky Way galaxy (approx. 100-400 billion).
The footprints left by Apollo astronauts on the Moon will remain there for at least 100 million years because there is no wind or water.
One million Earths could fit inside the Sun, which is a medium-sized star.
If two pieces of the same type of metal touch in space, they will permanently bond. This is known as cold welding.
There is a giant cloud of alcohol in Sagittarius B2, a gas cloud near the center of the Milky Way, containing billions of liters of ethanol.
The Sun accounts for 99.86% of all the mass in the entire Solar System.
Olympus Mons on Mars is the tallest volcano in the solar system. At 21 km (13.6 miles) high, it is three times the height of Mount Everest.
Light from the Sun takes exactly 8 minutes and 20 seconds to reach Earth.
Venus is the hottest planet in our solar system, with a surface temperature of around 450°C (840°F), hot enough to melt lead.
Jupiter's Great Red Spot is a giant hurricane-like storm that has raged for over 300 years and is larger than the Earth itself.
The Milky Way galaxy is estimated to be about 13.6 billion years old, almost as old as the Universe itself.
